requirement: Make securities exchange demo bulletproof, add Sprint 1-8 feature demos, fix reset, stabilize

tasks.title: Securities-service demo reset endpoint
tasks.description:
Add a POST /api/v1/securities/demo/reset endpoint that clears ALL in-memory state.

In src/securities-service/internal/server/:
1. Create handlers_demo.go with handleDemoReset(w, r).
2. The handler must call store.Reset() (or equivalent) on ALL stores in the Server struct. Since InMemory stores use a map, the simplest approach is to re-initialize them.
3. Add a Reset() method to each store interface in store.go (append to each interface). For InMemory implementations, Reset() locks the mutex, replaces the data map with a fresh empty map, and unlocks.
4. Register route: mux.HandleFunc("/api/v1/securities/demo/reset", s.handleDemoReset)
5. The handler should return 200 with {"status": "reset", "message": "All securities data cleared"}.

In src/gateway/cmd/gateway/main.go:
1. Add route: rt.Handle("POST", "/api/v1/securities/demo/reset", secHandler)
2. Add "/api/v1/securities/demo/reset" to public paths (no auth required, like the existing /api/v1/admin/demo/reset)

In src/demo-runner/src/data/sections.ts:
1. In the reset flow (useDemoRunner.ts), add a second reset call to /api/v1/securities/demo/reset after the existing auth reset call.

Go at /usr/local/go/bin/go. Build and test:
cd src/securities-service && go build ./... && go test ./...
cd ../gateway && go build ./...

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T1.md

tasks.title: Demo steps for Sprint 1-8 features
tasks.description:
Add new demo steps to src/demo-runner/src/data/sections.ts showcasing Sprint 1-8 features. These steps go AFTER the existing admin-ops section and BEFORE the readiness checklist.

Add a new section: 'Advanced Platform Features' (id: 'advanced-features') with these steps:

1. `adv-1`: Create Market Index — POST /api/v1/securities/indices
   body: {id: 'MSE-TOP20', name: 'MSE Top 20 Index', instrument_weights: {'{apu_id}': 0.6, '{gov_id}': 0.4}, base_value: 1000}
   Uses tenantHeader(state, 'admin'). Extract index_id.

2. `adv-2`: Calculate Index Value — POST /api/v1/securities/indices/{index_id}/calculate
   Uses tenantHeader(state, 'admin'). Validate response has current_value > 0.

3. `adv-3`: Create Instrument Folder — POST /api/v1/securities/folders
   body: {id: 'EQUITIES', name: 'Equities', parent_id: ''}
   Uses tenantHeader(state, 'admin').

4. `adv-4`: Create Sub-Folder — POST /api/v1/securities/folders
   body: {id: 'MINING', name: 'Mining Sector', parent_id: 'EQUITIES'}
   Uses tenantHeader(state, 'admin').

5. `adv-5`: List Folder Children — GET /api/v1/securities/folders/EQUITIES/children
   Uses tenantHeader(state, 'admin'). Validate MINING is in children.

6. `adv-6`: Set Entity Permissions — PUT /api/v1/securities/entity-permissions
   body: {role_id: 'TRADER', entity_type: 'INSTRUMENT', allow_create: false, allow_view: true, allow_edit: false, allow_delete: false, allow_approve: false}
   Uses tenantHeader(state, 'admin').

7. `adv-7`: View Entity Permissions — GET /api/v1/securities/entity-permissions?role_id=TRADER
   Uses tenantHeader(state, 'admin'). Validate permissions list is non-empty.

8. `adv-8`: Configure Trading Parameters — POST /api/v1/securities/trading-params
   body: {id: 'APU-PARAMS', instrument_id: '{apu_id}', name: 'APU Trading Parameters', min_order_size: 10, max_order_size: 100000, max_order_value: 500000000, short_selling_allowed: false}
   Uses tenantHeader(state, 'admin').

9. `adv-9`: View Surveillance Dashboard — GET /api/v1/securities/surveillance/dashboard
   Uses tenantHeader(state, 'admin'). Validate response has alert counts.

10. `adv-10`: List Warnings — GET /api/v1/securities/warnings?acknowledged=false
    Uses tenantHeader(state, 'admin'). Validate response is array.

All steps use the tenantHeader() helper. Each step validates the response status (200/201) and checks key response fields.

Also verify: existing sections still reference correct endpoints. Do NOT modify existing steps.

After editing sections.ts, run: cd src/demo-runner && npm test

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T2.md

tasks.title: Docker Compose health check & fix
tasks.description:
Verify Docker Compose stack starts cleanly and all key services are reachable.

1. Check current container status: docker compose ps
2. Check if securities-service (8089), gateway (8080), auth-service (8085), demo-runner (3002) are running
3. If not running, check logs: docker compose logs <service> --tail=50
4. Fix any build or startup issues
5. Verify health endpoints:
   - curl -s http://localhost:8080/healthz (gateway)
   - curl -s http://localhost:8089/healthz (securities-service direct)
   - curl -s http://localhost:8085/healthz (auth-service direct, if exists)
6. If services need rebuilding due to Sprint 8 code changes: docker compose build securities-service gateway
7. Verify the demo reset endpoint works: curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/securities/demo/reset
8. Verify a securities endpoint works: curl -s http://localhost:8089/api/v1/securities/instruments -H 'X-GarudaX-Tenant: mse-equities'

Document all findings and fixes in handoff.

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T3.md

tasks.title: Demo runner tests for new sections
tasks.description:
Add tests for the new demo steps and reset integration.

In src/demo-runner/src/__tests__/:
1. Test the new 'Advanced Platform Features' section exists and has 10 steps
2. Test each step has proper method, url, headers, body, and validateResponse
3. Test the reset flow calls both auth and securities reset endpoints
4. Test state extraction works (index_id from adv-1)

Run: cd src/demo-runner && npm test
All existing tests must still pass.

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T4.md
